message:
🌱 Add oras: artifactType in index manifest and in its "manifests" mission (#836)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "oras-1670-artifacttype-in-index-manifest-and-in-its-manifests-descriptors",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "oras: artifactType in index manifest and in its \"manifests\" descriptors",
+    "description": "artifactType in index manifest and in its \"manifests\" descriptors. Requested by 7+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check ORAS installation",
+        "description": "Verify your ORAS version:\n```bash\noras version\n```\nThis feature requires ORAS CLI installed locally. Install via `brew install oras` or download from https://oras.land/docs/installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Understand the feature context",
+        "description": "### What is the version of your ORAS CLI\n\n1.3.0-beta.2\n\n### What would you like to be added?\n\nI've been experimenting with using the new `oras manifest index create` command to construct multi-platform index manifests for the new (currently in"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for artifactType in index manifest and in its \"manifests\"\u2026",
+        "description": "This extends the previously-accepted proposal with two additional features related to the artifactType manifest property:\n\n- Both of the `oras manifest index` subcommands support an `--artifact-type` option, similar to that used with `oras push` today, that directly specifies an `artifactType`\n```json\n{\n  \"schemaVersion\": 2,\n  \"mediaType\": \"application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json\",\n  \"artifactType\": \"application/vnd.opentofu.provider\",\n  \"manifests\": [\n    {\n      \"mediaType\": \"application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json\",\n      \"artifactType\": \"application/vnd.opentofu.provider-target\",\n      \"digest\": \"sha256:f22b608064cba5d2bcea544ea636acca254ff3dcfcf7e39f6218b302c07c0a80\",\n      \"size\": 616,\n      \"platform\": {\n        \"architecture\": \"amd64\",\n        \"os\": \"linux\"\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"mediaType\": \"application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json\",\n      \"artifactType\": \"application/vnd\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the feature is working as expected:\n```bash\noras version\noras manifest fetch <registry>/<repo>:<tag>\n```\nConfirm that the feature described in \"artifactType in index manifest and in its \"manifests\" descriptors\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This extends the previously-accepted proposal with two additional features related to the artifactType manifest property:\n\n- Both of the `oras manifest index` subcommands support an `--artifact-type` option, similar to that used with `oras push` today, that directly specifies an `artifactType` value to include in the index manifest.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "{\n  \"schemaVersion\": 2,\n  \"mediaType\": \"application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json\",\n  \"artifactType\": \"application/vnd.opentofu.provider\",\n  \"manifests\": [\n    {\n      \"mediaType\": \"application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json\",\n      \"artifactType\": \"application/vnd.opentofu.provider-target\",\n      \"digest\": \"sha256:f22b608064cba5d2bcea544ea636acca254ff3dcfcf7e39f6218b302c07c0a80\",\n      \"size\": 616,\n      \"platform\": {\n        \"architecture\": \"amd64\",\n        \"os\": \"linux\"\n      }\n    },\n    {\n      \"mediaType\": \"application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json\",\n      \"artifactType\": \"application/vnd.opentofu.provider-target\",\n      \"digest\": \"sha256:9d4fa52874a3beb018d3c22683b3c49c2e2821a29c5479454958a59b5d8fee9b\",\n      \"size\": 616,\n      \"platform\": {\n        \"architecture\": \"arm64\",\n        \""
+      ]
+    }
+  },
